content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. The problem is in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()` function on line 65. 

The issue is that `this.treeNext` can be null when we're at the end of file (eof). When the file has a class without a body followed by blank lines and then end of file, the `treeNext` will be null, and calling `.elementType` on null causes the NPE.

Looking at the stack trace:
```
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode.getElementType()" because the return value of "org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode.getTreeNext()" is null
	at com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor(NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule.kt:65)
```

The fix is to add a null check for `treeNext` before accessing its `elementType`. We need to use safe call operator `?.` on `treeNext`.

import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

/**
 * Test for NPE fix when a class without body is followed by consecutive blank lines at end of file.
 * See: https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/...
 */
class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleNpeTest {
    private val noConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule() }

    @Test
    fun `Given a sealed class without body followed by consecutive blank lines at end of file then do not throw NPE`() {
        val code =
            """
            sealed class Hello



            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            sealed class Hello

            """.trimIndent()
        noConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Needless blank line(s)")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a class without body followed by consecutive blank lines at end of file then do not throw NPE`() {
        val code =
            """
            class Foo



            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            class Foo

            """.trimIndent()
        noConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Needless blank line(s)")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given an interface without body followed by consecutive blank lines at end of file then do not throw NPE`() {
        val code =
            """
            interface Bar



            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            interface Bar

            """.trimIndent()
        noConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleAssertThat(code)
            .hasLintViolation(3, 1, "Needless blank line(s)")
            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}

content.thinking:
The fix is in place. Let me summarize what was done:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The NPE was occurring in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()` function on line 65. When a class without body (like `sealed class Hello`) is followed by consecutive blank lines at the end of file, the `this.treeNext` is `null` because there's no next node after the whitespace. Calling `.elementType` on `null` caused the NullPointerException.

### The Fix
I changed line 65 from:
```kotlin
this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```
to:
```kotlin
this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```

The safe call operator `?.` ensures that when `treeNext` is `null`, the expression evaluates to `null == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR` which is `false`, instead of throwing an NPE.

### Testing
I created a new test file `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleNpeTest.kt` that specifically tests the case described in the issue (a sealed class without body followed by consecutive blank lines at end of file). The tests pass and verify that the rule correctly reports "Needless blank line(s)" without throwing an NPE.

Both the new tests and the existing tests pass successfully.

content.text:
## Summary

I've fixed the NPE issue in the `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule`. Here's what was done:

### Root Cause
The NPE was occurring in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()` function on line 65. When a class without body (like `sealed class Hello`) is followed by consecutive blank lines at the end of file, the `this.treeNext` is `null` because there's no next node after the whitespace node at EOF. Calling `.elementType` on `null` caused the NullPointerException.

### The Fix
Changed line 65 from:
```kotlin
this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```
to:
```kotlin
this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```

The safe call operator `?.` ensures that when `treeNext` is `null`, the expression evaluates to `null == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR` which is `false`, instead of throwing an NPE.

### Testing
Created a new test file `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleNpeTest.kt` that specifically tests the cases described in the issue:
- Sealed class without body followed by consecutive blank lines at EOF
- Regular class without body followed by consecutive blank lines at EOF  
- Interface without body followed by consecutive blank lines at EOF

All tests pass, and the existing tests also continue to pass.

11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId

14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
